reforms.description: The Czech Republic aims to better target R&D support specifically towards small and young dynamic firms to enhance their innovation capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeted R&D support can enhance the innovation capacity of SMEs, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to better target R&D support has been made as part of the policy insights.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how R&D funding is allocated, focusing on a critical segment of the economy that can drive innovation.
reforms.source_quote: R&D intensity is low, and R&D and innovation activity of SMEs is below par. Better target R&D support to small and young dynamic firms.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic plans to adopt a new Building Act to streamline the cumbersome procedures for obtaining construction permits and starting a business.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying the process for starting a business can encourage entrepreneurship and innovation, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Procedures to obtain construction permits and to start a business are cumbersome and lengthy. Adopt the new Building Act.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant barrier to business creation, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Procedures to obtain construction permits and to start a business are cumbersome and lengthy. Adopt the new Building Act and reduce the time and number of procedures for starting a business.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic aims to support R&D activities and innovation by providing assistance for cooperation between the research and business sectors, as well as facilitating the commercialization of R&D results.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D and innovation can lead to increased productivity and economic growth by fostering collaboration between research institutions and businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has prioritized support for R&D and innovation activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the innovation ecosystem in the Czech Republic, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Other key priorities of the current government include... Research, development and innovation – supporting R&D activities and innovation; providing support for cooperation between research and business sector and subsequent commercialisation of R&D results.

reforms.description: The COVID Technology Programme was launched to provide subsidies for projects aimed at combating the spread of coronavirus through the acquisition of new technological equipment and facilities, with a total budget of CZK 300 million.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By funding technological advancements, the programme aims to enhance the capacity of firms to innovate and respond to health crises, thereby supporting economic recovery.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was established with a defined budget to support technological projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant response to an urgent public health crisis, facilitating innovation in technology to address immediate challenges.
reforms.source_quote: COVID Technology Programme 19 (subsidy for projects directly linked to the fight against the further spread of coronavirus through the acquisition of new technological equipment and facilities, CZK 300 million in total)

reforms.description: The Czech Rise Up Programmes were initiated to support innovative companies, including start-ups, in developing new solutions to combat the coronavirus crisis, with a total budget of CZK 500 million.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to innovative firms, the programme aims to foster entrepreneurship and stimulate economic recovery through new business solutions.
reforms.status_evidence: The programmes were launched with allocated funding to support innovative responses to the crisis.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programmes are significant in their focus on supporting innovation during a critical period, enhancing the resilience of the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Czech Rise Up 1.0 and 2.0 Programmes (encouraging the introduction of new solutions to fight the coronavirus crisis by supporting innovative companies, including start-ups, CZK 500 million in total)

reforms.description: The Czech Republic is fostering R&D and innovation to improve its business environment, addressing the low R&D intensity and moderate innovation performance compared to EU peers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D and innovation is expected to raise productivity and competitiveness, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy is actively being pursued to enhance R&D and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards prioritizing R&D and innovation, which are critical for improving the overall productivity and competitiveness of the Czech economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Czech Republic is fostering R&D, innovation and improving the business environment.

reforms.description: The Czech government has prepared the Innovation Strategy of the Czech Republic 2019-2030, aiming to unify the design, assessment, and coordination of research, development, and innovation policies.
reforms.package_name: Innovation Strategy of the Czech Republic 2019-2030
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance the effectiveness of RDI policies, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has prepared the Innovation Strategy with ambitious goals.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant shift in how the Czech Republic approaches innovation policy, aiming for a comprehensive and coordinated framework.
reforms.source_quote: The government has also recently prepared the Innovation Strategy of the Czech Republic 2019-2030 with very ambitious goals.

reforms.description: The Czech government has implemented a reform of the Tax Incentives Act to simplify the procedure for companies claiming R&D tax allowances, aiming to better support business R&D spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying the tax incentive process encourages more firms to invest in R&D, potentially increasing overall R&D spending.
reforms.status_evidence: The change in R&D tax allowance came into effect in April 2019.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the existing tax incentive framework, making it easier for firms to access R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: Since April 2019, the change in R&D tax allowance came into effect.

reforms.description: The Czech government is preparing a comprehensive overhaul of the legislation and regulation around construction permits to streamline the process and reduce delays.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining construction permits can facilitate faster infrastructure development, benefiting startups and innovative firms needing quicker access to facilities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is preparing a comprehensive overhaul of the legislation and regulation around construction permits.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant barrier to business operations and investment, potentially leading to substantial economic benefits.
reforms.source_quote: The government is preparing a comprehensive overhaul of the legislation and regulation around construction permits.

reforms.description: The Czech government launched the Digital Czechia initiative to enhance e-government services, aiming to improve online interactions between citizens and public authorities, and to increase the competencies of public administration staff.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving digital services and administrative efficiency, the initiative is expected to enhance public service delivery and increase citizen engagement, which can lead to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative aims to provide user-friendly and efficient online services for citizens and businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to modernize public administration through digital transformation, which is crucial for improving service delivery and efficiency.
reforms.source_quote: The Digital Czechia initiative aims to provide user-friendly and efficient online services for citizens and businesses.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic plans to make more use of non-earmarked grants that require matched funding or to move to block grants, which are less constraining, to boost innovation and efficiency in local governance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing earmarking, municipalities can allocate resources more efficiently, fostering innovation and improving service delivery.
reforms.status_evidence: The Czech Republic could therefore make more use of non-earmarked grants that require matched funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy that could enhance local governance efficiency and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The Czech Republic could therefore make more use of non-earmarked grants that require matched funding or to move to block grants, which are less constraining, as in Denmark and Norway.
